name: Carriage 4 — The Library
summary: A reading room paneled in walnut that has no business existing inside a moving train, with the child sitting in the corner as if she has been waiting.

notable_features: Shelves run floor to ceiling on all four walls, broken only by the doors. The spines are uniform brown cloth, blank. When you tilt your head, the wheels go quieter, as if the books are listening too.
A single book lies open on the central reading desk, weighted by a brass page-holder. The text inside is in alternating columns: one in plain English, one in a cipher the eye does not settle on.
The child sits in the alcove between the two front shelves. Her feet do not quite touch the floor. She holds the bear with both hands, the way you hold something that you are afraid will speak.

Against the side wall is a glass display case. Inside, on red velvet, is a single metal fragment the length of a finger. The case has no lock visible, only a slot the shape of a column heading from the open book.
The reading lamp nearest the child does not flicker. None of the others have stopped flickering since you entered.

san_triggers.trigger_text: The wheels go quieter on this side of the carriage. The books are doing the listening.
When you read a line from the cipher, the next line is already what you were about to think.
The bear's button eyes are on you. The child's are not.

skill_check_hooks.action_hint: scan the shelves for the one book that is different
work through the cipher in the open book
examine the display case for the key-shape

puzzle.description: The cipher in the open book names a single letter-shape; that shape is the slot in the display case. Decode, then turn.
puzzle.unlock_condition: Player solves the cipher via a Library Use (regular) success AND retrieves the key-fragment item by matching the shape in the display case (Spot Hidden regular).
